Items.Title: 数字 SaaS 和传统营销渠道实践 | Digital SaaS and Traditional Marketing Funnel Practices
Items.Description:
营销漏斗的概念自 1898 年就已存在，但这个概念多年来一直在演变。 传统的营销渠道在很长一段时间内运作良好，但现代技术对其产生了巨大影响。

传统营销漏斗

传统的营销漏斗围绕着意识、兴趣和欲望。 客户必须意识到存在一种产品，并且必须对获得该产品感兴趣。 他们还需要实际进行购买的愿望。

在这个营销时代，比较购物是一个现实，但它主要受地域限制。 如果顾客去一家商店购买产品，他们可能会比较货架上的不同选项，但他们不太可能走出那家商店去完全寻找另一种选择。

数字营销 SaaS 漏斗

互联网改变了一切。 比较购物不再局限于单一商店或地理区域； 客户足不出户就可以访问来自几十个不同国家的数百种竞争产品。

这催生了更精明的客户，他们对他们感兴趣的品牌提出了更多要求。

反过来，品牌也在进化。 他们开始直接与客户接触，创造了一种以前不存在的对话。

有如此多的选择，品牌不得不开始以新的方式争夺客户的注意力。 这催生了数字营销漏斗，一种专注于品牌参与、教育和拥抱基于互联网的客户体验购物行为的非线性方式的方法。

主要区别

传统营销漏斗和数字营销漏斗之间有两个主要区别。 首先是客户以不同的方式体验渠道。 在互联网出现之前，客户倾向于以相同的顺序体验营销渠道。

现在，客户可以无序地体验漏斗的各个阶段，甚至可以完全跳过各个阶段。 人们需要更加个性化的体验，而营销渠道已经适应了这种需求。
第二个主要区别是更加关注品牌/客户关系。

客户越来越了解营销和“被卖给”，因此品牌必须更加努力地确保他们与客户的互动是积极的、真实的和有价值的。

创建您自己的数字 SaaS 营销渠道

在设计您自己的数字营销计划时，从客户的角度了解数字营销漏斗阶段非常重要。 您可以根据他们的要求使用不同的数字营销策略，例如联盟营销计划、 品牌大使计划、谷歌广告和许多其他策略。

虽然客户可能会在不同的订单中遇到数字营销渠道的各个阶段，甚至可能会完全跳过各个阶段，但营销人员应该花时间了解每个阶段及其在数字营销销售渠道中的作用。

以下阶段按最常见的顺序列出。 其中六个阶段——参与、教育、研究、评估、论证和购买——发生在销售点之前和销售点之前。 最后四个阶段——采用、保留、扩展和宣传——发生在销售点之后。

这些阶段与预售阶段一样重要； 一旦客户购买了产品或服务，数字营销人员的工作就没有完成。

订婚
在客户接触您的产品之前，他们可能有机会接触您的品牌。 数字营销渠道的这个阶段关注确保客户与您的品牌的互动是积极的，并且他们对未来的互动持开放态度。

对您的网站和其他营销活动进行定期CRO 审核，以修复错误并从您的所有数字营销销售渠道中获得良好的结果。

许多品牌使用 Facebook 和 Twitter 等社交媒体网站来吸引潜在客户。 这些网站的性质使品牌能够接触到大量人群，虽然并非所有这些人都会成为客户，但它们可以帮助对您的品牌产生积极的影响。

品牌为他们的网站创建有创意的谷歌评论小部件，以在他们的网站上显示他们的本地列表。 它增加了访问者对您本地列表的信任，您也可以从本地列表中获得好的列表。

教育
内容营销的目的之一是教育：具体来说，就是帮助潜在客户意识到他们有问题，而您可以为他们解决这些问题。 此阶段通常不关注特定产品，而是关注识别和解释问题并讨论不同的潜在解决方案。 您可以为您的在线访问者创建有效的弹出窗口来订阅您的时事通讯。

研究
一旦客户意识到问题，下一步通常不仅要寻找解决方案，还要寻找解决问题可以使客户受益的方法。 在这一点上，市场营销的重点仍然不是销售产品，而是帮助客户确定他们如何从解决问题中获益。

继续以施工经理为例，向客户介绍使用无人机和机器人所涉及的成本节省或工作现场安全性的提高可以帮助施工经理得出结论，这是一个值得解决的问题。
评估
此时，是时候提出问题的具体解决方案了：产品。 客户可能会针对他们的问题寻找多个相互竞争的解决方案，因此您的重点应该是向客户展示为什么您的产品是他们的最佳解决方案。

介绍价值主张，解释您产品的优势，将其成本与竞争对手进行比较，并帮助客户对您的产品感到满意，认为这是一个很好的解决方案。

理由
并非所有潜在客户都会成为实际客户。 在许多情况下，您会成功地说服潜在客户您的解决方案是可用的最佳解决方案，但您仍然无法完成销售。

将价格监控工具添加到您的网站，以便您可以为您的客户提供更好的报价，并向您的客户提供这方面的教育。 它将帮助您建立信任并为您的客户提供更好的价值。

论证阶段是关于寻找克服异议、障碍或惯性的方法。 也许客户不是有能力做出购买决定的人。

也许客户只是对现状满意，解决问题并不是最优先考虑的事情。 向客户提供优先考虑的原因或他们可以用来说服那些有购买力的人的信息。

购买
购买阶段就是销售。 确保客户对购买感到满意，您已经回答了他们的所有问题，并且他们对您的产品将为他们提供的价值充满信心。

采用
采用——数字 SaaS 营销渠道的第一个购买后阶段——需要兑现你的承诺。 在购买阶段，您让客户感到放心，因为他们做出了正确的决定。 现在是您实现这一目标的时候了，以便客户对您的产品有良好的体验。

保留
满意的客户成为回头客。 要留住客户，请在他们需要时提供帮助，并为他们提供有关如何充分利用您的产品的教育材料。

扩张
如果您到目前为止一直做得很好，您现在可以开始扩大初始销售。 这可能意味着向客户销售额外的产品或服务，升级他们的服务，或者让他们对解决不同问题的完全不同的产品感兴趣。

达到这个阶段的关键是帮助客户认为您的品牌是可靠的，并且是您销售的产品和您解决的问题的权威。

倡导
极其满意的客户可以帮助您进一步扩大客户群。 他们成为品牌拥护者，并参与将您的产品销售给他们的同行、同事和朋友。

当客户成为品牌拥护者时，让他们对您的产品保持满意和兴奋是非常重要的。 毕竟，他们会以一种或另一种方式谈论它； 您应该确保他们对您的品牌说好话。



原文:

The idea of a marketing funnel has existed since 1898, but the concept has evolved over the years. The traditional marketing funnel worked well for a long time, but modern technology has affected it dramatically.

Traditional Marketing Funnel

The traditional marketing funnel revolved around awareness, interest, and desire. Customers had to be aware that a kind of product existed and had to be interested in obtaining the product. They also needed the desire to actually make the purchase.

Comparison shopping was a reality during this era of marketing, but it was primarily limited by geography. If customers went to a store to buy a product, they might compare the different options on the shelf, but they were unlikely to go beyond that store to find another option entirely.

Digital Marketing SaaS Funnel

The internet changed everything. Comparison shopping was no longer limited to a single store or geographic region; customers could access literally hundreds of competing products from dozens of different countries without leaving their homes.

This gave rise to savvier customers who demanded more from their brands of interest.

Brands, in turn, evolved. They started engaging directly with customers, creating a dialogue that didn’t exist previously.

With so many options available, brands had to start competing for customer attention in new ways. This gave rise to the digital marketing funnel, a method that focused on brand engagement, education, and embracing the non-linear way in which internet-based customers experienced the act of shopping.

Major Differences

There are two major differences between the traditional marketing funnel and the digital marketing funnel. The first is that customers experience the funnel in a different way. Before the internet, customers tended to experience the marketing funnel in the same order.

Now, customers can experience stages of the funnel out of order, or even skip phases entirely. People demand a more customized experience, and the marketing funnel has adapted to that demand.
The second major difference is an increased focus on the brand/customer relationship.

Customers have become more aware of marketing and being “sold to,” so brands have to work harder to ensure that their interactions with customers are positive, authentic, and valuable.

Creating Your Own Digital SaaS Marketing Funnel

When designing your own digital marketing plan, it’s important to understand the digital marketing funnel stages from the customer’s perspective. You can use different digital marketing strategies such as affiliate marketing program, brand ambassador program, Google Ads, and many other strategies as per their requirements.

While customers can encounter stages of the digital marketing funnel in different orders and may even skip stages entirely, marketers should spend time understanding each stage and its role in the digital marketing sales funnel.

The stages below are listed in the most common order. Six of these stages — engagement, education, research, evaluation, justification, and purchase — take place before and up to the point of sale. The last four stages — adoption, retention, expansion, and advocacy — occur after the point of sale.

These stages are just as important as the pre-sale stages; a digital marketer’s job isn’t done once the customer has bought the product or service.

Engagement
Before customers even encounter your product, they may have an opportunity to encounter your brand. This stage of the digital marketing funnel is concerned with ensuring that customers’ interactions with your brand are positive and that they’re open to future interactions.

Make regular CRO audit on your websites and other markting activities to get fix the errors and get good results from your all digital markting sales funnel.

Many brands use social media sites like Facebook and Twitter to engage with potential customers. The nature of these sites allows brands to reach a large number of people and, while not all of those people will become customers, they can help generate positive buzz about your brand.

Brands create creative Google review widget for their websites to show their local listing over their website. It increase the trust among the visitors towards your local listing and you can get good list from local listing as well.

Education
One of the purposes of content marketing is education: specifically, helping potential customers realize that they have a problem that you can solve for them. This stage doesn’t typically focus on specific products, but rather on identifying and explaining the problem and talking about different potential solutions. You can create effective pop ups for your online visitors to subscribe your newsletters.

Research
Once a customer is aware of a problem, the next step is typically looking not only for solutions, but for ways that solving the problem can benefit the customer. At this point, marketing still isn’t focused on selling a product, but rather on helping customers identify how they can benefit from solving the problem.

To continue the construction manager example, teaching the customer about the cost savings involved in using drones and robots or about the increase in job site safety can help that construction manager come to the conclusion that it’s a problem worth solving.
Evaluation
At this point, it’s time to present a specific solution to the problem: the product. Customers may be looking at multiple competing solutions to their problem, so your focus should be on showing customers why your product is their best solution.

Introduce value propositions, explain the benefits of your product, compare its cost with your competitors, and help the customer become comfortable with your product as a good solution.

Justification
Not all potential customers become actual customers. In many cases, you’ll succeed at convincing a potential customer that your solution is the best one available, but you still won’t make the sale.

Add a price monitoring tool to your website so that you can create better offers for your customers and also educate on this to your customers. It will help you generate trust and deliver better value to your customers.

The justification stage is about finding ways to overcome objections, obstacles, or inertia. Perhaps the customer isn’t the one with the ability to make buying decisions.

Maybe the customer is simply fine with the status quo, and solving the problem isn’t a high priority. Provide customers with reasons why it is a priority or with information they can use to convince those with buying power.

Purchase
The purchase stage is all about the sale. Make sure customers are comfortable with the purchase, that you’ve answered all their questions, and that they’re confident in the value your product will provide for them.

Adoption
Adoption — the first post-purchase stage of the digital SaaS marketing funnel — necessitates making good on your promises. During the purchase stage, you made customers comfortable that they were making a good decision. Now it’s time for you to deliver on that so the customer has a good experience with your product.

Retention
Satisfied customers become repeat customers. To retain customers, give them help when they need it and provide them with educational materials on how to get the most out of your product.

Expansion
If you’ve been doing a good job up to this point, you can now start to expand the initial sale. This might mean selling customers additional products or services, upgrading their service, or getting them interested in a completely different product that solves a different problem.

The key to reaching this stage is helping the customer see your brand as dependable and an authority on the products you sell and the problems you solve.

Advocacy
Extremely satisfied customers can help you expand your customer base further. They become brand advocates and do part of the work of selling your product to their peers, colleagues, and friends.

When customers become brand advocates, it’s incredibly important that they remain satisfied with and excited about your product. After all, they’re going to be out there talking about it one way or another; you should make sure they’re saying good things about your brand.

Items.Title: Angular Material 菜单组件的无缝指南 | A seamless guide on Angular Material Menu Component
Items.Description:
好吧，这是一个简单的浮动菜单，其中包含各种菜单选项，并带有指向应用程序各个页面的适当链接。 我们还可以根据项目要求在工具栏、抽屉和页脚中使用菜单。

在本文中，我们将构建一个包含许多选项的菜单，下面将对其进行快速说明。

什么是材质菜单组件？

材质菜单是一个包含选项列表的浮动面板。

在 Angular 中，我们可以应用 MatMenuModule 角度材质菜单模块来构建菜单项。

该元素本身不呈现任何内容。

目录

第一步：导入角材质菜单模块

材料菜单模块可以包含在我们的组件 ts 文件、app.module.ts 文件以及整个应用程序中使用的任何其他常用材料模块中。

 import { MatMenuModule } from "@angular/material/menu";

步骤：2：使用垫子菜单选择器显示菜单项

在我们的组件代码中导入 MatMenuModule 之后，使用 mat-menu 选择器，它只不过是一个包含菜单选择列表的浮动面板。

 <mat-menu menu="materialMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item</button><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item</button> </mat-menu>

作为菜单项，我使用的是按钮元素。

并且不要忘记将 mat-menu-item 添加到菜单选项中。

在 mat-menu 变量中，添加一个模板引用变量，用于引用我们的菜单面板。

在这种情况下，我包含了变量菜单。

 <mat-menu menu="materialMenu"> </mat-menu>

第 3 步：添加 matMenuTriggerFor 元素。

上面的 mat-menu 元素在用户界面上没有渲染。

菜单应绑定到使用 matMenuTriggerFor 指令打开和关闭菜单的触发器元素。

简单的菜单：

 <button mat-button="">Simple Menu</button> <mat-menu menu="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item 2</button> </mat-menu>

更改垫子菜单位置

材质菜单将默认显示在菜单触发元素上方。

如果您希望菜单显示在菜单触发元素之前或之后，请将 xPosition 值设置为“之前”或“之后”。

如果您只想让菜单出现在菜单触发元素之前或之后，请将 xPosition 值设置为“之前”或“之后”。

将 yPosition 属性设置为“上方”以在菜单触发元素上方显示菜单。

阅读更多：创建可重用的 Angular 组件

<button mat-button="">Before</button> <mat-menu beforemenu="matMenu" xposition="before"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button> </mat-menu><button mat-button="">After</button> <mat-menu aftermenu="matMenu" xposition="after"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button> </mat-menu><button mat-button="">Above</button> <mat-menu abovemenu="matMenu" yposition="above"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button> </mat-menu><button mat-button="">Below</button> <mat-menu belowmenu="matMenu" yposition="below"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button> </mat-menu>


如何创建嵌套菜单？

到目前为止，我们已经创建了一个带有菜单项的简单菜单，但是如果我们有多个类别的菜单项呢？

如果我们在单个应用程序中有大量页面，那么嵌套菜单将很有用，因此我们希望用户能够从单个页面访问应用程序的所有页面。

有了素材菜单，我们就有权自定义子菜单项的嵌套菜单； 只需按照基本步骤操作，我们就会有一个嵌套的材质菜单。

创建嵌套菜单或子菜单非常简单。

使用 matmenutriggerFor 属性，我们必须向 mat-menu-item 添加另一个菜单。

如何禁用 mat-menu-item？

寻找可靠的AngularJS 开发公司？

带有图标的垫子菜单的另一个例子：

我们在前面的例子中开发了一个简单的菜单，但在这个例子中我们也可以使用不同的材质图标和菜单和菜单项。 只需按照下面概述的说明来完成此操作。

我将在这个例子中使用各种材料图标，所以我们需要一个这样的样式表。

结论

在本文中，我们学习了如何通过逐步过程将材质菜单组件与我们的 Angular 应用程序集成。 我们还介绍了更改垫子菜单的位置，以及如何实际启用和禁用它。



原文:

The Angular Material Menu component is used to display a menu with several menu items which can be used to navigate from one page to another. Well, it's a simple floating menu that contains various menu selections with appropriate links to the application's various pages. We can also use menus inside toolbars, drawers, and footers depending on the project requirements.

In this article, we will construct a menu with many options, that will be quickly explained below.

What is the material menu component?

The Material menu is a floating panel containing a list of options.

In Angular, we may apply the MatMenuModule angular material menu module to build menu items.

The element does not render anything on its own.

Table of contents

Step:1: Import angular material menu module

A material menu module can be included in our components ts file, app.module.ts file, and any other common material module used throughout the application.

import { MatMenuModule } from "@angular/material/menu";

Step:2: Use a mat menu selector to display menu items

Use mat-menu selector, which is nothing more than a floating panel containing a list of menu selections, after importing MatMenuModule in our component code.


<mat-menu menu="materialMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item</button><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item</button>
 </mat-menu>

As a Menu Item, I'm using the button element.

And do not forget to add mat-menu-item to the menu options.

In the mat-menu variable, add a template reference variable which will be used to refer to our menu panel.

In this scenario, I've included the variable menu.


<mat-menu menu="materialMenu"> </mat-menu>

Step:3 : Add matMenuTriggerFor element.

The above mat-menu element has no render on the user interface.

The mat-menu is a directive that is used to trigger the menu options when the button click event is triggered because it cannot open menu items by itself.

The menu should be tied to a trigger element that uses the matMenuTriggerFor directive to open and shut the menu.

Simple menu:


<button mat-button="">Simple Menu</button>
<mat-menu menu="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Menu Item 2</button>
</mat-menu>

Changing mat menu position

The material menu will be displayed by default above the menu trigger element.

If you want the menu to display before or after the menu trigger element, set the xPosition value to "before" or "after."

Set the xPosition value to "before" or "after" if you just want the menu to appear before or after the menu trigger element.

The xPosition attribute sets the menu all along the horizontal axis.

The yPosition attribute is used to change the menu's vertical position.

Set the yPosition property to "above" to display the menu just above the menu trigger element.

The values "above" and "below" are accepted by yPosition.

Read More: Creating Reusable Angular Components 


<button mat-button="">Before</button>
<mat-menu beforemenu="matMenu" xposition="before"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button>
</mat-menu><button mat-button="">After</button>
<mat-menu aftermenu="matMenu" xposition="after"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button>
</mat-menu><button mat-button="">Above</button>
<mat-menu abovemenu="matMenu" yposition="above"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button>
</mat-menu><button mat-button="">Below</button>
<mat-menu belowmenu="matMenu" yposition="below"><button mat-menu-item="">Item 1</button><button mat-menu-item="">Item 2</button>
</mat-menu>

[output:3]

[output:5]

How to create Nested menus?

So far, we've created a simple menu with menu items, but what if we have several categories of menu items?

The nested menu will be useful if we have a big number of pages in a single application so we want the user to be able to access all of the application's pages from a single page.

With a material menu, we have the right to customize a nested menu with the child menu items; just follow the basic steps and we will have a nested material menu.

It is quite simple to create nested menus or sub-menus.

Using the matmenutriggerFor attribute, we have to add another menu to the mat-menu-item.


<button mat-button="">Animal index</button>
<mat-menu animals="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Vertebrates</button><button mat-menu-item="">Invertebrates</button>
</mat-menu>
<mat-menu vertebrates="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Fishes</button><button mat-menu-item="">Amphibians</button><button mat-menu-item="">Reptiles</button><button mat-menu-item="">Birds</button><button mat-menu-item="">Mammals</button>
</mat-menu>
<mat-menu invertebrates="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Insects</button><button mat-menu-item="">Molluscs</button><button mat-menu-item="">Crustaceans</button><button mat-menu-item="">Corals</button><button mat-menu-item="">Arachnids</button><button mat-menu-item="">Velvet worms</button><button mat-menu-item="">Horseshoe crabs</button>
</mat-menu>
<mat-menu fish="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Baikal oilfish</button><button mat-menu-item="">Bala shark</button><button mat-menu-item="">Ballan wrasse</button><button mat-menu-item="">Bamboo shark</button><button mat-menu-item="">Banded killifish</button>
</mat-menu>
<mat-menu amphibians="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Sonoran desert toad</button><button mat-menu-item="">Western toad</button><button mat-menu-item="">Arroyo toad</button><button mat-menu-item="">Yosemite toad</button>
</mat-menu>
<mat-menu reptiles="matMenu"><button mat-menu-item="">Banded Day Gecko</button><button mat-menu-item="">Banded Gila Monster</button><button mat-menu-item="">Black Tree Monitor</button><button mat-menu-item="">Blue Spiny Lizard</button><button disabled="disabled" mat-menu-item="">Velociraptor</button>
</mat-menu>

[app.component.html]


[output:6]


[output:7]

How to disable mat-menu-item?

The disabled property can be used to disable the mat menu item.

I've added a property called IsDisabled to the MatMenuListItem object described above. It can also be used to turn off the menu link.


<button mat-menu-item="" ngfor="let item of menuListItems">
<mat-icon>{{ item.menuIcon }}</mat-icon>
<span> {{ item.menuLinkText }}</span></button>

[app.component.html]

Searching for Reliable AngularJS Development Company?

Another example of mat-menu with icon:

In this example, I'll use one menu with an icon;

We developed a simple menu in our previous example, but we can also use different material icons with menu and menu items in this example. Simply follow the instructions outlined below to accomplish this.

Items.Title: 如何在特定区域对网站进行排名 | How to rank a web site on a specific region
Items.Description:
塔里克庄园

发表于 2 月 13 日

在特定地区对网站进行排名涉及针对该地区的搜索引擎优化网站。 您可以采取以下一些步骤来提高网站在特定地区的知名度和排名：

  • 进行关键字研究：确定与特定地区的目标受众相关的关键字和短语。 您可以使用 Google Keyword Planner 等工具来研究目标区域中最流行的关键字。

  • 针对本地搜索优化您的网站：通过在您的网站上包含您的公司地址、电话号码和 Google 地图，确保您的网站针对本地搜索进行了优化。 您还应该在标题标签、标题和内容中包含特定于位置的关键字。

  • 创建本地列表：使用本地目录（例如 Google 我的商家、Bing Places for Business 和目标区域中的其他相关目录）注册您的公司。 这些列表有助于搜索引擎了解您的业务位置，还可以提高您在本地搜索结果中的可见度。

  • 获取本地反向链接：来自目标区域网站的反向链接有助于提高网站在该地区的知名度和可信度。 联系当地企业和组织，看看他们是否愿意链接到您的网站。

  • 使用架构标记：架构标记是您可以添加到您的网站以帮助搜索引擎了解您网站上的信息的代码。 使用架构标记来指示您的公司地址、电话号码和其他相关信息，例如营业时间、评论和评级。

  • 监控您的进度：定期监控您网站的性能并根据需要进行更改以提高您的知名度和排名。 您可以使用 Google Analytics 等工具来跟踪您网站的流量并查看它在您的目标区域中的表现。

通过遵循这些步骤并不断努力提高网站的知名度和排名，您可以增加在特定区域获得良好排名的机会。 请记住，搜索引擎优化是一个持续的过程，随着搜索算法和用户行为的变化，您可能需要随着时间的推移进行调整。

Tarik Manoar

Posted on Feb 13

Ranking a website in a specific region involves optimizing the site for search engines in that region. Here are some steps you can take to improve your website's visibility and ranking in a specific region:

  • Conduct keyword research: Identify the keywords and phrases that are relevant to your target audience in the specific region. You can use tools like Google Keyword Planner to research the most popular keywords in your target area.

  • Optimize your website for local search: Make sure your website is optimized for local search by including your business address, phone number, and a Google Map on your website. You should also include location-specific keywords in your title tags, headings, and content.

  • Create local listings: Register your business with local directories such as Google My Business, Bing Places for Business, and other relevant directories in your target area. These listings help search engines understand the location of your business and can also improve your visibility in local search results.

  • Get local backlinks: Backlinks from websites in your target area can help improve your website's visibility and credibility in that region. Reach out to local businesses and organizations to see if they would be willing to link to your site.

  • Use schema markup: Schema markup is code that you can add to your website to help search engines understand the information on your site. Use schema markup to indicate your business address, phone number, and other relevant information, such as business hours, reviews, and ratings.

  • Monitor your progress: Regularly monitor your website's performance and make changes as necessary to improve your visibility and ranking. You can use tools like Google Analytics to track your website's traffic and see how well it is performing in your target area.

By following these steps and continually working to improve your website's visibility and ranking, you can increase the chances of ranking well in a specific region. Keep in mind that search engine optimization is an ongoing process, and you may need to make adjustments over time as search algorithms and user behavior change.

Items.Title: Azure Developer CLI Azure Developer CLI 第 0.6.0 集 - 我着迷了 | Azure Developer CLI Azure Developer CLI episode 0.6.0 - I am hooked
Items.Description:
您可以在上面的目录中找到以前的版本👆

现在是二月初，这意味着……（短促的鼓声）……Azure Developer CLI 的新月度版本已经发布！ 所以欢迎版本0.6.0-beta2 。

与上个月相同的过程：让我们看看我们需要在 Azure Functions 项目中更改什么，以便从版本 0.5.0-beta.3 移动到当前最新和最好的版本 0.6.0-beta.2。

发生了什么变化？

像往常一样，未来几周可能会发布一些更新的补丁版本来修复一些故障（第一个已经发布），但新闻是 0.6.0-beta.1 版本的一部分。

该版本附带了一些不错的新功能，使azd更加成熟和生产就绪，例如对多个 Azure 组织的支持和许多新的azd模板（参见此处- 我在那里选择了我最喜欢的，但我有点偏见😜）。

很酷的是，没有结构上的变化使您有必要重构您的项目以保持完全兼容和最新。 在你停止阅读之前，请继续关注我：有些事情可能会改进你的项目，我将重点关注它们以及我在升级到版本 0.5.0-beta3 时遇到的粗糙边缘。

因此，我不会遍历每一个更改，但强烈建议您自己遍历发行说明，因为您可能会在设置中使用 gem。

安装/更新

上次Windows上azd CLI的升级有点坎坷。 我猜这是由于转向基于 MSI 的 Windows 安装。 这确实是一个错误，并且在这个版本中得到了修复。 因此，与往常一样，CLI 版本的升级非常顺利，没有任何问题。 我使用azd CLI 的 GitHub README.md 中描述的 PowerShell 命令进行升级。

对于 MacOS 用户，您现在还可以使用winget或brew来安装和升级您的 CLI。

我没有尝试过，因为我个人仍然没有设法在 Windows 上使用一个包管理器。 到目前为止，由于缺少软件包，我总是需要使用 Chocolatey 来补充winget - 我最近没有检查，我已经习惯了“安装混乱”。

登录体验

版本 0.5.0-beta.1 删除了 Azure CLI 对登录 Azure 帐户的依赖。 但是，如果您有个人 Azure 帐户，则该流程不起作用。 如果是个人帐户，您必须通过配置azd config set auth.useAzCliAuth true切换回azd的旧行为。

对于所有需要该解决方法的用户来说，这是个好消息- 版本 0.6.0-beta.1 修复了该行为，并且登录流程对个人帐户也很有吸引力。 这意味着您可以从以下位置删除解决方法和依赖项：

  • 通过azd config unset auth从你的azd配置
  • 请注意，在两种情况下，您仍然必须保持对 Azure CLI 的依赖性才能执行登录流程，即：

    • 当您将 Azure DevOps 用于 CI/CD 并使用随azd提供的管道配置时
    • 当您使用 Terraform 作为基础架构即代码时。 此差距是由于此处所述的 Terraform 提供程序造成的，并在azd文档中突出显示。

    到目前为止，这看起来不错。 现在让我们切换到 0.6.0-beta.1 版本中最大的新功能，命令和服务挂钩。

    新功能 - 命令和服务挂钩

    当读到这些新的“hooks”时，我想到了一首歌……如果你看过银河护卫队，你就会知道我在说什么——Blue Swede 的“Hooked on a feeling”？

     Ooga-Chaka Ooga-Ooga [...] I'm HOOKED on a feeling I'm high on believing That you're in love with me [...]

    有了这个摆动，让我们深入探讨这个话题本身。 通常，此功能通过启用azd工作流的进一步自定义为您提供额外的自由度。 这意味着您可以在azd命令执行的某些时刻添加自定义逻辑。 你为什么需要那个？ 有时您可能想要执行额外的脚本任务，例如，在您的基础设施配置完成之后。 有了这个版本，您可以做到这一点。

    您可以在命令/服务生命周期事件之前和之后挂钩您的自定义逻辑。 您可以在文档中找到受支持命令的完整列表。

    这听起来像是一个有用的功能。 我在 Azure Functions 项目中不需要这些挂钩，但我在另一个项目中有它，所以为什么不试一试并尝试一下。

    钩子在azure.yaml文件中定义如下：

     name: todo-nodejs-mongo metadata: template: todo-nodejs-mongo@0.0.1-beta hooks: prerestore: shell: sh run: echo 'Hello' preprovision: shell: sh run: ./hooks/preprovision.sh services: web: project: ./src/web dist: build language: js host: appservice api: project: ./src/api language: js host: appservice

    如我们所见，我们在hooks部分指定了 hooks 并定义：

    • 触发钩子的事件
    • 应该使用的外壳
    • 以及应该直接作为字符串或脚本执行的代码

    看起来并不太复杂。 让我们尝试使用公告博客文章和文档中的示例，看看它是如何工作的。

    示例代码 - 哎呀

    获取示例代码并将其应用于 PowerShell 会产生以下azure.yaml ：

     # yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta hooks: preinit: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host 'Hello from preinit hook' preprovision: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host 'Hello from preprovision hook' services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function infra: path: infra

    但这会导致编辑器出现验证错误：

    验证源自raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-d…中可用的 JSON 模式。

    我本以为至少会切换到 1.1 版，但事实并非如此。 至少在升级到新的azd版本时不需要调整任何东西。

    查看 JSON 模式的相应部分，您将看到没有为azd init命令定义的挂钩。 也许只是 JSON 架构中的一个问题，而azd CLI 知道该事件。 但是在用上面的azure.yaml尝试命令之后，也没有迹象表明钩子被识别了。 好的，示例代码的小问题。

    让我们尝试另一个事件，即preinfradelete 。

    互动还是非互动——这是个问题

    我调整了azure.yaml以执行preinfradelete挂钩：

     # yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta hooks: preinfradelete: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preinfradelete hook" services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function infra: path: infra

    尝试此配置会导致：

    显然该钩子已被识别（并可能被执行），但“Hello from the preinfradelete hook”输出在哪里？ 救援文件！

    为事件定义配置时还有两个选项：

    • 接下来尝试：

       # yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta hooks: preinfradelete: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preinfradelete hook" interactive: true services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function infra: path: infra

      结果：

      成功了。 应添加到相应代码示例中的内容。 但首先要庆祝我们这边的成功。

      让我们尝试更多的东西。

      查看可能的挂钩，我们发现我们可以有一个用于azd provision和一个用于azd up 。 由于azd up是一个命令来统治它们，我想到的问题是：如果我为事件preprovision和preup配置一个钩子，它们会被执行还是只触发up事件，因为这个事件被否决其他事件？

      让我们试试看：

       # yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta hooks: preprovision: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from prepreprovision hook" interactive: true preup: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preup hook" interactive: true services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function infra: path: infra

      结果：

      所有挂钩都被执行，并且它们在preup事件是第一个事件的情况下按顺序执行。 记住这一点很重要，以避免重复执行活动或脚本。

      接下来我们可以尝试什么？ 挂钩允许挂钩配置的操作系统相关定义，让我们看一下。

      操作系统依赖

      根据代码示例，您可以通过以下方式在定义挂钩时区分操作系统：

       name: todo-nodejs-mongo metadata: template: todo-nodejs-mongo@0.0.1-beta hooks: preinit: posix: # Only runs on Posix environments shell: sh run: echo 'Hello' windows: # Only runs on Windows environments shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello" services: web: project: ./src/web dist: build language: js host: appservice api: project: ./src/api language: js host: appservice

      将其应用到我的项目中并尝试使用 Windows 我的azure.yaml如下所示：

       name: azure-functions-blob metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta hooks: preprovision: windows: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from the preprovision hook" interactive: true preinfradelete: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preinfradelete hook" interactive: true services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function infra: path: infra

      至于preinit场景，编辑器显示了一个验证问题：

      嗯……检查 JSON 模式，原因就很清楚了：

       "definitions": { "hook": { "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": [ "run" ], "properties": { "shell": { "type": "string", "title": "Type of shell to execute scripts", "description": "Optional. The type of shell to use for the hook. (Default: sh)", "enum": [ "sh", "pwsh" ], "default": "sh" }, "run": { "type": "string", "title": "Required. The inline script or relative path of your scripts from the project or service path", "description": "When specifying an inline script you also must specify the `shell` to use. This is automatically inferred when using paths." },

      操作系统依赖项是挂钩定义的一部分，因此即使在指定操作系统时，验证也会因缺少顶级run密钥而失败。

      与preinit相比，操作系统依赖性本身是架构的一部分，因此让我们检查azd CLI 是否可以通过执行它来处理不一致的azure.yaml文件。

      结果是：可以，所以问题只是需要更正的 JSON 模式。

      这很好，因为这样所有开发人员在使用azd时都会感到被包括在内，无论他们在什么操作系统上运行。 这是非常感谢的，因为我经常为没有 Mac 而苦恼，并且在工具中经常将它们视为一等公民。

      配置后如何获取变量

      好的，既然我们现在了解了钩子的机制，我还有一个问题。 如果我想在配置基础设施后执行一些额外的操作，我如何才能获得相关信息，例如azd流运行时端点的 URL？

      在azd执行期间，此信息是否可作为环境变量使用？ 让我们通过添加在postprovison挂钩中执行的 PowerShell 脚本来找出它：

       # yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta hooks: preprovision: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from the preprovision hook" interactive: true postprovision: shell: pwsh run: hooks/postprovisionhook.ps1 interactive: true services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function infra: path: infra

      该脚本列出了当时可用的所有环境变量：

       #!/usr/bin/env pwsh Write-Host "Here are the env variables after provisioning" Get-ChildItem Env: | Sort-Object Name | Format-Table -Wrap

      我们发现以下数据可用于我们的脚本：

      • 来自.azure/<your project name>/.env数据可通过环境变量获得
      • 很高兴知道这一点，尤其是对于我之前提到的我需要这些数据的其他项目。

        话虽如此，我从main.bicep重新定义了我的输出名称，以便具有一致的前缀：

         output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_KEY_VAULT_ENDPOINT string = keyVault.outputs.endpoint output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_APPINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING string = monitoring.outputs.applicationInsightsConnectionString output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_FUNCTION_URL string = function.outputs.FUNCTION_URI output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_AZURE_LOCATION string = location output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_AZURE_TENANT_ID string = tenant().tenantId

        我不知道这是否有意义或从长远来看有帮助。 时间和利用钩子的项目会证明一切。

        好消息是azd GitHub 存储库中已经有关于此主题的未解决问题。 你在这里找到它。 似乎计划在 4 月份发布azd 。

        最佳实践和示例

        挂钩是扩展和配置azd的一种非常强大的方法。 尽管如此，我相信，这种巨大的灵活性会带来巨大的责任。

        考虑到这一点，从如何构建钩子开始，获得一些关于钩子使用的指导和最佳实践可能会很棒。 示例代码指向使用专用目录来收集脚本。 关于命名约定、脚本在不同环境中的使用等方面的最佳方法是什么？

        示例/模板中的一些示例将有助于查看实际中的最佳实践，但也有助于了解什么是典型用例以及您可能不应该在挂钩中做什么。

        由于挂钩的发布是全新的，我认为在接下来的几周内，利用挂钩的示例/模板以及如何最好地使用它们的指南将会出现。

        对于这篇博文，还有一件事值得一提，即现在包含在azd init命令中的git init 。

        正如您从发行说明中看到的那样， azd init command现在可以恢复文件的可执行权限并自动初始化git 存储库。

        这意味着git init是初始化过程的一部分：

        我喜欢这个有用的增强功能。 话虽如此，我认为如果通过询问用户是否应该在初始化之后执行初始提交（包括提交消息的查询）来使流程更进一步，那也会很棒。 这将允许在使用模板时有一个干净的起点。

        这应该是流程中的一个可选步骤，这似乎不会太远，因为azd的GitCli接口已经有一个在管道中使用的Commit函数。 也许这也可以集成到azd init流程中。

        带着这个愿望，是时候总结一下了

        概括

        我认为此版本通过引入挂钩对azd进行了巨大的功能增强。 我喜欢挂钩的方法和设计，从我的角度来看，它们增加了很多价值。

        但是，文档和示例需要跟上azd CLI 的创新速度，才能让入门体验顺畅。 但我乐观地认为，这将在接下来的几周和几个月内发生。

        当然，还有许多其他不错的添加和改进，例如在 UI 中，每个月都是如此：感谢azd团队！

        从兼容性的角度来看，如果使用个人 Azure 帐户，除了删除登录的解决方法外，基本上不需要做任何事情。

        我已经迷上了azd ，现在我更迷上了它（不是最好的双关语，但我无法阻止它 - 请耐心等待😁）。

        期待在我的项目中充满“真实”生活，当然也像往常一样好奇azd团队接下来会发生什么。

        在哪里可以找到代码

        您可以在此处找到项目的代码和描述（注意分支）：

        最新最好的项目代码在主分支中，可能与本博文中描述的代码有所不同。

原文:

⚠ Advice - This blog post is part of a journey with the Azure Developer CLI and the journey has started three blog posts earlier - in case you missed them it might make sense to shortly catch-up otherwise this one might be a bit out of context.
You find the previous editions in the table of contents above 👆

It is the beginning of February and that means ... (short drum roll) ... the new monthly version of the Azure Developer CLI is out! So welcome version 0.6.0-beta2.

So same procedure as last month: let us see what we would need to change in our Azure Functions project to move from version 0.5.0-beta.3 to the currently latest and greatest version 0.6.0-beta.2.

What has changed?

📝 Remark: I will focus on version 0.6.0-beta.1. As usual there might be some newer patch releases coming in the next weeks to fix some glitches (and the first one is already out), but news are part of the 0.6.0-beta.1 release.

The release comes with some nice new features making azd more mature and production ready like the support Support for multiple Azure organizations and a lot of new azd templates (see here - I have picked my favorite there, but I am a bit biased 😜).

The cool thing is that there are no structural changes that make it necessary to refactor your project to stay fully compatible and up to date. Before you stop reading, stay with me: there are some things that might improve your project and I will focus on them as well as on the rough edges that I came across when upgrading to version 0.5.0-beta3.

Hence, I will not walk through each and every change but highly recommend to walk-through the release note yourself as there might be gems you can use in your setup.

Installation/Update

Last time the upgrade of the azd CLI on Windows was a bit bumpy. I guess that was due to the move towards the MSI based installation for Windows. This was indeed a bug and it was fixed with this release. So as usual the upgrade of the CLI version went smooth without any hazel. I used the PowerShell command described on the GitHub README.md of the azd CLI to do the upgrade.

You can now also use winget or brewfor MacOS users to install and upgrade your CLI.

I did not try that out, as I personally still did not manage to use one single package manager on Windows. Up to now I always needed to use Chocolatey complementing winget due to missing packages - I did not check lately, I live with the "installation mayhem" I am used to.

The login experience

Version 0.5.0-beta.1 removed the dependency from the Azure CLI for the login to the Azure account. However, that flow did not work if you had a personal Azure account. In case of personal account you had to switch back to the old behavior of azd by the configuration azd config set auth.useAzCliAuth true.

Good news for all the users that needed that workaround - version 0.6.0-beta.1 fixed the behavior and the login flow works like a charm also for personal accounts. This means you can remove the workaround and the dependency from:

  • from your azd configuration via azd config unset auth
  • the .devcontainer/devcontainer.json file
  • the GitHub Action workflow file .github/workflows/azure-dev.yml

Be aware that there are two scenarios where you still must keep the dependency to the Azure CLI to execute the login flow namely:

  • when you are using Azure DevOps for CI/CD and use the pipeline configuration that is delivered with azd
  • when you are using Terraform as Infrastructure-as-Code. This gap is due to the Terraform provider as described here and also highlighted in the azd documentation.

Up to now that looks good. Now let us switch to the biggest new feature that comes with version 0.6.0-beta.1, the command and service hooks.

New Feature - command and service hooks

When reading about these new "hooks" one song came to my mind ... if you saw Guardians of the Galaxy you know what I am talking about right - "Hooked on a feeling" by Blue Swede?

Ooga-Chaka Ooga-Ooga
[...]
I'm HOOKED on a feeling
I'm high on believing
That you're in love with me
[...]

With this swing let us dive in the topic itself. In general, this feature gives you additional degrees of freedom by enabling further customization of the azd workflow. This means that at certain points in the azd command execution you can add custom logic. Why would you need that? Sometimes you might want to execute additional scripted tasks e.g., after your infrastructure was provisioned. With this release you can do that.

azd distinguishes two categories of hooks namely command hooks and service lifecycle hooks. You can hook your custom logic before and after the command/service lifecycle event. You find the complete list of the supported commands in the documentation.

📝 Remark: The list of commands in the announcement blog post is not complete (the *infradelete events are missing for example), so I recommend to refer to the documentation to get the complete list.

That sounds like a useful feature. I do not have the necessity of these hooks in the Azure Functions project but I have it in another project, so why not give it a spin and play a bit around with them.

The hooks are defined in the azure.yaml file like this:

name: todo-nodejs-mongo
metadata: template: todo-nodejs-mongo@0.0.1-beta
hooks: prerestore: shell: sh run: echo 'Hello' preprovision: shell: sh run: ./hooks/preprovision.sh
services: web: project: ./src/web dist: build language: js host: appservice api: project: ./src/api language: js host: appservice

As we can see we specify the hooks in a hooks section and define:

  • the event that triggers the hook
  • the shell that should be used
  • and the code that should be executed either directly as string or as script

Looks not too complicated. Let us try it with the samples from the announcement blog post and the documentation and see how things work.

The sample code - oops

Taking the sample code and adopting it to PowerShell results in the following azure.yaml:

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob
metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preinit: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host 'Hello from preinit hook' preprovision: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host 'Hello from preprovision hook'
services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function
infra: path: infra

But that leads to a validation error in the editor:

The validation originates form the JSON schema available at raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-d….

📝 Remark: I was a bit surprised that the version of the JSON schema did not change despite the additional content. I would have expected at least a switch to version 1.1, but this is not the case. At least nothing that needs to be adjusted when upgrading to the new azd version.

Looking into the corresponding section of the JSON schema, you will see that the there are no hooks defined for the azd init command. Maybe just an issue in the JSON schema and the azd CLI know about the event. But also after trying out the command with the azure.yaml from above, no hint that the hook was recognized. Okay so small issue with the sample code.

Let us give it a try with another event namely preinfradelete.

Interactive or not interactive - that's the question

I adjusted the azure.yaml to execute the preinfradelete hook:

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob
metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preinfradelete: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preinfradelete hook"
services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function
infra: path: infra

Giving this configuration a try results in:

Obviously the hook was recognized (and maybe executed), but where is the "Hello from the preinfradelete hook" output? Documentation for the rescue!

There are two more options when defining the configuration for an event namely:

  • interactive: When set will bind the running script to the console stdin, stdout and stderr (default false).
  • continueOnError: When set will continue to execute even after a script error occurred during a command hook (default false).

The interactive: true is needed otherwise you will not see the output. Next try with:

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob
metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preinfradelete: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preinfradelete hook" interactive: true
services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function
infra: path: infra

Result:

That did the trick. Something that should be added to the corresponding code samples. But first success to celebrate on our side.

Let us try out some more things.

azd up - one command to rule them all, but what about the hook

Looking at the possible hooks we see that we can have one for azd provision and one for azd up. As azd up is the one command to rule them all, the question that came to my mind is: if I configure a hook for the event preprovision and preup, will they both be executed or will just the up event be fired as this one overrules the other events?

Let us try it out:

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob
metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preprovision: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from prepreprovision hook" interactive: true preup: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preup hook" interactive: true
services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function
infra: path: infra

Result:

All hooks are executed and they are executed sequentially where the preup event is the very first one. This is important to keep in mind to avoid duplicate executions of activities or scripts.

What can we try out next? The hooks allow a OS dependent definition of the hook configuration, let us take a look at that.

OS dependency

According to the code samples you can distinguish the OS when defining the hook via:

name: todo-nodejs-mongo
metadata: template: todo-nodejs-mongo@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preinit: posix: # Only runs on Posix environments shell: sh run: echo 'Hello' windows: # Only runs on Windows environments shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello"
services: web: project: ./src/web dist: build language: js host: appservice api: project: ./src/api language: js host: appservice

Adopting that to my project and just giving Windows a try my azure.yaml looks like this:

name: azure-functions-blob
metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preprovision: windows: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from the preprovision hook" interactive: true preinfradelete: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from preinfradelete hook" interactive: true services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function
infra: path: infra

And as for the preinit scenario the editor shows a validation issue:

Hmmm ... checking the JSON schema the reason for that becomes clear:

 "definitions": { "hook": { "type": "object", "additionalProperties": false, "required": [ "run" ], "properties": { "shell": { "type": "string", "title": "Type of shell to execute scripts", "description": "Optional. The type of shell to use for the hook. (Default: sh)", "enum": [ "sh", "pwsh" ], "default": "sh" }, "run": { "type": "string", "title": "Required. The inline script or relative path of your scripts from the project or service path", "description": "When specifying an inline script you also must specify the `shell` to use. This is automatically inferred when using paths." },

run is required and defined on top level. The OS dependencies are part of the hook definition, so even when specifying the OS the validation fails due to a missing top-level run key.

In contrast to preinit, the OS dependency per se is part of the schema, so let us check if the azd CLI can deal with the inconsistent azure.yaml file by executing it.

The result is: It can, so the issue is just the JSON schema that needs to be corrected.

That's good as this way all developers feel included when using azd no matter what OS they are running on. This is highly appreciated as I often struggle with not having a Mac and at tooling often treats them as first class citizen.

How do I get the variables after provisioning

Okay so as we now know the mechanics of the hooks, I have one more question. If I want to do some additional actions after provisioning the infrastructure, how can I get to the relevant information, like the URL of an endpoint when the azd flow is running?

Is this information available as environment variables during the execution of azd? Let us find it out by adding a PowerShell script that is executed in the postprovison hook:

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Azure/azure-dev/main/schemas/v1.0/azure.yaml.json name: azure-functions-blob
metadata: template: azure-functions-blob@0.0.1-beta
hooks: preprovision: shell: pwsh run: Write-Host "Hello from the preprovision hook" interactive: true postprovision: shell: pwsh run: hooks/postprovisionhook.ps1 interactive: true
services: blob-output-binding: project: src language: ts host: function
infra: path: infra

The script lists all environment variables available at that point in time:

#!/usr/bin/env pwsh

Write-Host "Here are the env variables after provisioning"

Get-ChildItem Env: | Sort-Object Name | Format-Table -Wrap

The execution of azd provison picks up the hook and shows that they are indeed available as environment variables:

We find the following data that we can use for our script:

  • The data from .azure/<your project name>/.env is available via environment variables
  • the data defined as output in the main.bicep file is available via environment variables

That is good to know especially for the other project I mentioned before where I need this data.

Having said that I redefined the names of my outputs from the main.bicep in order to have a consistent prefix:

output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_KEY_VAULT_ENDPOINT string = keyVault.outputs.endpoint
output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_APPINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING string = monitoring.outputs.applicationInsightsConnectionString
output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_FUNCTION_URL string = function.outputs.FUNCTION_URI
output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_AZURE_LOCATION string = location
output AZD_FUNC_BLOB_AZURE_TENANT_ID string = tenant().tenantId

I do not know if this makes sense or helps on the long run. Time and a project leveraging the hooks will tell.

📝 Remark: Some additional documentation would be great on this topic. The good news is that there is already an open issue the azd GitHub repository on this topic. You find it here. Seems to be planned for the April release of azd.

Best practices and samples

The hooks are a very powerful way to extend and configure azd. Nevertheless I am convinced that with this great power of flexibility comes great responsibility.

With that in mind it might be great to get some guidance and best practices for the usage of hooks starting in how to structure them. The sample code points to use a dedicated directory for collecting the scripts. What is the best way to go from there with regards to things like naming conventions, usage of scripts in different environments etc.

Some examples in the samples/templates would be useful to see the best practices in action, but also to get some ideas what are typical use cases and maybe what you should not do in the hooks.

As the release of the hooks is brand-new, I assume that over the course of the upcoming weeks samples/templates leveraging hooks and guidelines how to use them best will follow.

For this blog post there is one more thing that is worth to mention, namely the git init that is now included in the azd init command.

Automatic initialization of a git repository

As you can see from the release notes the azd init command now restores file executable permissions and initializes a git repository automatically.

This means that a git init is part of the initialization process:

I like that useful enhancement. Having said that I think it would also be great if the flow is taken one step further by querying the user if a initial commit should be executed after the initialization including the query for a commit message. That would allow a clean starting point when working with the templates.

This should be an optional step in the flow and this seems not to be too far away as the GitCli interface of azd already has a Commit function that is used in the pipelines. Maybe this could also be integrated into the azd init flow.

With this wish, time for a summary

Summary

I think that this release contains a huge functional enhancement of the azd by introducing hooks. I like the approach and the design of the hooks and from my perspective they add a lot of value.

However, the documentation and the samples need to catch up with the speed of innovation of the azd CLI to make the start a smooth experience. The CLI is already very feature rich and good documentation helps a lot to start with the CLI but also to make use of maybe "hidden" useful features. But I am optimistic that this will happen over the next weeks and months.

Of course there are also a lot of other nice additions and improvements like in the UI, so as every month: kudos to the azd team!

From the point of view of compatibility, there is basically nothing you need to do except to remove the workaround for the login if using personal Azure accounts.

I was already hooked to azd, now I am hooked even more to it (not the best pun, but I could not hold it back - bear with me 😁).

Looking forward filling the hooks with "real" life in my projects and of course as usual curious what comes next from the azd team.

Where to find the code

You find the code and the description of the project here (mind the branch):

github.com/lechnerc77/azd-compatibility…

The latest and greatest project code is in the main branch which might deviate from the code described in this blog post.

Useful references

Useful references if you want to try things out on your own:

Items.Title: 运动控制快速入门（三）| 如何开发运动控制器ZPLC程序 | Motion Control Quick Start (3) | How to Develop Motion Controller ZPLC Program
Items.Description:
我们知道，前面介绍了快速入门（一）——固件更新和快速入门（二）——ZBasic程序开发。 此外，还有 3 种编程语言，ZBasic、ZPLC 和 ZHMI。 那么，今天就来学习一下如何通过ZPLC进行运动控制的开发。

首先，什么是ZPLC ？

此外，它还支持与 ZBasic 的混合编程。 此外，还有一些不同的指令，即增加了一些新的指令，如触点逻辑运算指令、浮点数触点比较指令、ZPLC调用ZBasic指令、注释指令//等。

现在，我们已经对ZPLC有了基本的了解，接下来，通过下面的文字和图片说明，详细了解如何通过ZPLC进行开发。

一、运动控制器ZPLC程序开发代码

 //axis parameters initialization  LD M8002  ZRST M0 M10  LD M8002  BASE k0 k1  EXE @UNITS= 100,100  EXE @ATYPE = 1,1  EXE @SPEED=200,200  EXE @ACCEL=1000,1000  EXE @DECEL=1000,1000  EXE @SRAMP=200,200  EXE @DPOS=0,0  EXE @MPOS=0,0  EXE @FASTDEC=20000,20000  //linear interpolation  LDP X0  DEMOV K300 D0  DEMOV K400 D2  // IN1 rising edge is on and runs, IN 2 is pressed to stop motion rapidly  LDP X1  OR M0  ANI M1  ANI X2  OUT M0  EXEP @TRIGGER  MOVE D0 D2  LD M8100  PLS M1  END

二、具体步骤

首先在PC中新建一个文件夹，用来保存要制作的工程。 然后，打开ZDevelop软件，点击“文件”-“新建工程”，出现一个“另存为”界面，选择打开建好的文件夹，输入文件名，保存工程，新工程就做好了。 请注意文件后缀为“.zpj”。

点击“文件”-“新建文件”。

选择文件类型（PLC），然后单击“确定”。

可见Basic、PLC、HMI混合编程是有效的。

双击“AutoRun”，输入任务号为0。

有两种方法。

如果指令格式正确，光标会跳到下一个窗格，如果不正确，则会弹出一个窗口。

指令可以直接输入，也可以从下拉列表或左侧常用指令列表中选择。

程序编辑完成后，点击下方标记保存文件。

而新建的基础文件会自动保存到工程zpj下的文件中。

编辑好程序，然后点击“控制器”——“连接”。 如果没有controller，点击“controller”——“connect to simulator”。 而这个例程选择的是模拟器，连接时有“Connected to Controller:ZMC4xx-Simu Version:4.80-20180104”。

当弹出“connect to controller”窗口时，可以选择串口或网口。

点击下面两个按钮，RAM 表示下载到 RAM，ROM 表示下载到 ROM。 程序下载完成后，“输出和命令”窗口也会有提示，同时程序会自动运行。

即下次连接控制器时，下载到ROM 中的程序将根据任务号自动运行。

点击“Debug”--“Start/Stop Debug”调用“task and watch”窗口。

任务窗口用于查看运行状态和任务行号。 观察窗用于观察轴参数、自定义变量、输入输出状态等。

单击“查看”--“范围”打开相应窗口。

打开“Config”设置采集通道数、深度、空间等需要的参数，然后点击“OK”。

在这里，选择 5 个通道：

曲线2——0轴的VP_SPEED（插补合并速度）

曲线 3 -- 轴 0 的 MSPEED（子矢量速度）

曲线 4 -- 轴 1 的 MSPEED（子矢量速度）

然后，单击“开始”按钮。

闭合X0 上升沿触点，将轴0 和轴1 的移动距离参数传送到数据寄存器中存储。

闭合X1触点产生上升沿脉冲，M0导通并自锁，同时触发示波器采样命令和MOVE直线插补运动命令的执行。 下面是波形图，X2 触点用于快速停止轴运动。

轴运行时，按X2键，得到如下图形，插补轴以之前设置的FASTDEC速度停止。

关于智动

就这些了，感谢您的阅读——运动控制快速入门（三）| 如何开发运动控制器ZPLC程序

更多信息请关注“支持”和“下载” ，关于Zmotion还有其他平台- Youtube & LinkedIn & Twitter & Tiktok & Facebook，包括技术信息（开发环境，例程代码），产品展示，公司发展等

希望认识你，和你聊天，和你成为朋友。 欢迎！

本文由ZMOTION编辑，在此，分享给大家，一起学习。

智动科技专注于运动控制技术和通用运动控制产品的开发，是国家高新技术企业。 由于在运动控制技术上的专注和耕耘，中动通讯已成为国内发展最快的工业运动控制公司之一，也是国内少有的完全掌握运动控制核心技术和实时工控软件的公司。 此外，还有一款由Zmotion开发的程序软件——ZDevelop。 是您编程编译的不错选择。 并通过上位机编程，有上位机说明书。

原文:

As we know, quick start (1) -- firmware update and quick start (2) -- ZBasic program development are introduced before. Also, there are 3 kinds of programming language, ZBasic, ZPLC and ZHMI. Then, today, let's learn how to develop motion control through ZPLC.

Firstly, What's the ZPLC?

ZPLC is the PLC language supported by Zmotion motion controller, it can use ladder of diagram (LAD) and statement list (STL) to program, which means it compatible with most PLC commands, for customers who are familiar to PLC can rapidly program. In addition, it supports hybrid programming with ZBasic. What's more, there are some different instructions, namely, some new instructions are added, such as, contact logic operation instructions, floating-point number contact comparison instructions, ZPLC calling ZBasic instructions, comment instructions //, etc.

Now, we know ZPLC basically, next, to learn how to develop through ZPLC for details from below words and image description.

1. Motion Controller ZPLC Program Development Code 

    //axis parameters initialization
　　LD M8002
　　ZRST M0 M10
　　LD M8002
　　BASE k0 k1
　　EXE @UNITS= 100,100
　　EXE @ATYPE = 1,1
　　EXE @SPEED=200,200
　　EXE @ACCEL=1000,1000
　　EXE @DECEL=1000,1000
　　EXE @SRAMP=200,200
　　EXE @DPOS=0,0
　　EXE @MPOS=0,0
　　EXE @FASTDEC=20000,20000
　　//linear interpolation
　　LDP X0
　　DEMOV K300 D0
　　DEMOV K400      D2
　　// IN1 rising edge is on and runs, IN 2 is pressed to stop motion rapidly
　　LDP X1
　　OR M0
　　ANI                     M1
　　ANI                     X2
　　OUT M0
　　EXEP         @TRIGGER
　　MOVE  D0  D2
　　LD   M8100
　　PLS   M1
　　END

2. Specific Steps

(1) new build the project

Firstly, new build one folder in PC to save the project to be made. Then, open ZDevelop software, click "File" - "New Project", one "save as" interface appears, select the built folder to open, and enter the file name, save the project, now new project is made. Please note the file suffix is ".zpj".

(2) new build the file

Click "File" - "New File".

Select the file type (PLC), and click "OK".

It can be seen hybrid programming among Basic, PLC and HMI is valid.

(3) set file as automatic operation

Double click "AutoRun", and input task Number as 0.

(4) edit the program

There are two methods.

--select the pane, directly enter instruction, operand and operand No., then click "ok".

If the instruction format is correct, the cursor will jump to next pane, but if it is incorrect, there will pop up one window.

--directly click the "symbol" in LD tool bar, then, "Emter PLC Instruction" window pops up, also, input instruction, operand and operand No.

The instruction can be input directly, or can be selected from drop-down list or from left commonly used instructions list.

When the program is edited, click below mark to save the file.

And new built basic file will be saved automatically into the file under project zpj.

(5) connect to controller

Edit the program well, then click "controller" -- "connect". If there is no controller, click "controller" -- "connect to simulator". And this routine selects simulator, when connected, there is "Connected to Controller:ZMC4xx-Simu Version:4.80-20180104."

When "connect to controller" window popped up, serial port or net port can be selected.

(6) download the program

Click following two buttons, RAM means download into RAM, ROM means download into ROM. When the program is downloaded, also, there is message in "output and command" window, at the same time, the program will run automatically.

RAM supports power failure storage, but ROM doesn't support. That is, the program that was downloaded into ROM will run automatically according to task number when the controller is connected next time.

(7) start to debug

Click "Debug" -- "Start/Stop Debug" to call "task and watch" window.

Task window is used to check running status and task line number. Watch window is used to watch axis parameters, customized variables, input and output status, etc.

(8) oscilloscope sampling

Click "View" -- "Scope" to open the relative window.

Open "Config" to set captured channel numbers, depth, space and other required parameters, then click "OK".

Here, select 5 channels:

curve 0 -- DPOS of axis 0 (the position)

curve 1 -- DPOS of axis 1 (the position)

curve 2 -- VP_SPEED of axis 0 (the interpolation merge speed)

curve 3 -- MSPEED of axis 0 (sub vector speed)

curve 4 -- MSPEED of axis 1 (sub vector speed)

Then, click "start" button.

Close the X0 rising edge contact, and transfer the movement distance parameters of axis 0 and axis 1 to the data register for storage.

Close the X1 contact to generate a rising edge pulse, M0 conducts and self-locks, at the same time triggers the execution of the oscilloscope sampling command and the MOVE linear interpolation motion command. Below is the waveform, X2 contact is used to stop axis motion rapidly.

The trajectory under XY mode:

When axis is running, press X2 button, and gain following graphics, interpolation axis stops at the speed of FASTDEC set before.

ABOUT ZMOTION

That's all, thank you for your reading -- Motion Control Quick Start (3) | How to Develop Motion Controller ZPLC Program

For more information, please pay close attention to "Support" and "Download" , and there are other platforms about Zmotion - Youtube & LinkedIn & Twitter & Tiktok & Facebook, including technical information (development environment, routine code), product showing, company development, etc.

Hope to meet you, talk with you and be friends with you. Welcome!

This article is edited by ZMOTION, here, share with you, let's learn together.

ZMOTION: DO THE BEST TO USE MOTION CONTROL.

Zmotion Technology focuses on development of motion control technology and general motion control products, it is a national high and new technology enterprise. Due to its concentration and hard work in motion control technology, ZMOTION already become one of the fastest growing industrial motion control companies in China, and is also the rare company who has managed core technologies of motion control and real time industrial control software completely. Here, Zmotion provides motion controller, motion control card, vision motion controller, expansion module and HMI. In addition, there is one program software developed by Zmotion -- ZDevelop. It is a good choice for you to program and compile. And program through upper computer, there is PC manual.
